lines.text: PAGE TWO
AIRPORT AND UPON DETERMINING KITES NOT PRACTICAL HAD ABANDONED THE PROJECT
[REDACTED] STATED HE HAD TAKEN SIXTEEN MM PICTURES WHICH ARE PRESENTLY BEING
DEVELOPED. STATED WOULD MAKE PICTURES AND PARTS FROM OBJECT AVAILABLE
TO THIS OFFICE. [REDACTED] DESCRIBED OBJECT AS APPROXIMATELY SIX FEET IN DIA
METER, CONSISTING OF DISK MOUNTED ON AXEL WITH BEARINGS, WINGS
ON EITHER SIDE, TAIL SURFACE SUPPORTED ON BOOMS, CUP MOUNTED
ON EACH WING TIP RESEMBLING ROCKET POWER CONTAINERS. [REDACTED] STATED DID
NOT OBSERVE ANY CHEMICAL OR OTHER MATTERIAL IN CONTAINERS WHICH COULD BE
USED AS SELF PROPELLING FORCE. MR. [REDACTED] MAN-
UFACTURING COMPANY ADVISED [REDACTED] FINANCED BY THEIR COMPAN
IN DEVELOPING TOY KITE, THAT [REDACTED] HAD PROACHED IDEA OF MAKING LARGE
MODEL TO BE USED AS TOW TARGET FOR AIRPLANES BUT [REDACTED] DECIDED
THAT HE MAKE ONLY KITE SUITABLE FOR SALE AS TOYS. [unclear] HOWEVER, b7c
[REDACTED] MADE LARGE MODEL APPROXIMATELY SIX FEET IN DIAMETER, WEIGHING
FIFTY POUNDS, WHICH TOGETHER WITH OTHER MODEL HAD BEEN FLOWN IN DC THREE
OWNED BY [REDACTED] TO PRIVATE AIRPORT OF [REDACTED] AND
[REDACTED] COULD NOT RECALL EXACT DATE, BUT [unclear] STATED [unclear] IT
WAS IN EARLY PART OF DEC. [REDACTED] STATED DIFFICULTIES ENCOUNTERED IN
FLIGHT OF KITE, AND THAT IN VIEW OF SIZE OF LARGE MODEL IT WAS NECESSARY
TO TOW IT BEHIND PLANE TO OBSERVE FLIGHT. STATED MODEL CRASHED AND [REDACTED]
AND [REDACTED] ABANDONED EXPERIMENT LEAVING AIRPORT WITH [REDACTED] REMAINING BE-
HIND TO CONDUCT FURTHER EXPERIMENTS. [REDACTED] STATED DID NOT KNOW THAT WOL-
FERT HAD LEFT THE LARGE MODEL AT THIS AIRPORT UNTIL ADVISED BY [REDACTED]
END PAGE TWO
